burial.plot_notes: Memorial burial at Weybridge Hill Cemetery VT; KIA at Savage's Station VA.

sources.short: University of Vermont — Henry Harrison Wilder Papers
sources.notes: 4 folders of Civil War correspondence 1861-1862. Letters from Henry to family + condolence letters + Sergeant Merrill Samson account of his death.

military_service.unit: Company F, 5th Regiment, Vermont Volunteer Infantry
military_service.notes: Enlisted Sept 19, 1861 in Weybridge VT as a corporal in Company F, 5th Vermont Volunteer Infantry. Wrote home about Camp Holbrook (St. Albans VT) and Camp Griffin (Virginia) where the regiment was hit hard by disease. Described the Peninsula Campaign in spring 1862, including the battles of Lee's Mill and Williamsburg. Killed in action at Battle of Savage's Station (Henrico County VA) on Jun 29, 1862, age ~22. Best friend Sergeant Merrill Samson of Weybridge wrote a letter to Henry's family describing his death.

narrative_blocks.body: Cpl. Henry Harrison Wilder of Weybridge VT enlisted in the 5th Vermont Volunteer Infantry, Company F, on Sept 19, 1861 at age 21. He survived the disease-ridden winter at Camp Griffin VA, then marched through the Peninsula Campaign — Lee's Mill and Williamsburg. On June 29, 1862, during the Seven Days Battles, his regiment was thrown into the Battle of Savage's Station in Henrico County VA.

narrative_blocks.header: His mother saved his Civil War letters
narrative_blocks.body: Henry Harrison Wilder's mother Amanda Murray Hayward saved his correspondence from the war along with Sgt. Merrill Samson's account of her son's death and the letters of condolence the family received. The collection — four folders spanning 1861-1862 — is now at the University of Vermont Special Collections (Henry Harrison Wilder Papers). Amanda is the principal correspondent in the archive.

burial.plot_notes: Memorial burial at Weybridge Hill Cemetery VT; KIA at Savage's Station VA.

burial.plot_notes: Weybridge Hill Cemetery, Weybridge, Addison County VT (per Findagrave). Same cemetery as son Henry Harrison Wilder (HD3219, KIA buried memorial).

narrative_blocks.header: First husband abandoned the family, then vanished
narrative_blocks.body: Amanda's first husband Orlando Wilder — biological father of her two children Marion Eliza (born 1836) and Henry Harrison (born 1840) — last appeared in the 1840 Addison County Vermont census and then disappeared from all records. He had either abandoned the family or moved to Minnesota alone, leaving Amanda widow-like at age thirty with two young children. The disappearance was permanent.

narrative_blocks.header: Remarried Judge Joseph Hayward with three stepchildren
narrative_blocks.body: Between 1840 and 1850 Amanda remarried — to Judge Joseph Hayward of Vermont (1796-1865). Hayward already had three children from his prior marriage (Asaph, Josephine, and Benjamin); they were Amanda's stepchildren, not her own biological children. The blended Wilder-Hayward household took shape across the 1840s, with Amanda raising her two Wilder children alongside her three Hayward stepchildren.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter Marion married into Drake family
narrative_blocks.body: Amanda's daughter Marion Eliza Wilder (born 1836) eventually married Drake — establishing the Wilder-Drake line that descended from the Hooker connection through Amanda's mother Lucinda Murray. Amanda's son Henry Harrison Wilder served as a private in the Civil War.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Addison County VT 1810, died Vermont 1887
narrative_blocks.body: Amanda Murray was born April 30, 1810 in Addison County Vermont and died December 9, 1887 in Vermont at age seventy-seven. Her two marriages with their distinct biological-vs-step parentage have been cross-checked against source records to establish the marriage order and parentage definitively.

burial.plot_notes: Town Line Cemetery, Bridport, Addison County VT (per Findagrave). Same as wife Polly.
Town Line Cemetery, Bridport, Addison County VT (per Findagrave). Same cemetery as her father Deacon Ichabod Bartlett Jr (X00205) and her sister Marina Bartlett Wright (HD3189).

burialCemetery.name: Weybridge Hill Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Burial place of Judge Joseph Hayward (X01947), Amanda Murray Hayward (X01943), Marion Eliza Wilder Drake (X01952), PVT Henry Harrison Wilder (X01953).
